summary: The Docker-based reproduction cloned the pinned vulnerable Heimdall commit, mounted it into the verifier container, and confirmed that a 64 MiB form body on the default body-token extraction path causes large transient and retained heap growth.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should show that a 64 MiB application/x-www-form-urlencoded body containing access_token is fully buffered and decoded, yielding BODY_BYTES=67108864, TOKEN_BYTES=67108864, retained heap above 120000000 bytes, and total allocations above 200000000 bytes.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit b0716ebc564ad2205179a39978b795f5db4870c5, built the verifier image, and produced BODY_BYTES=67108864, TOKEN_BYTES=67108864, HEAP_ALLOC_AFTER=201473200, HEAP_ALLOC_AFTER_GC=134364000, HEAP_ALLOC_DELTA=201326616, HEAP_ALLOC_DELTA_AFTER_GC=134217416, and TOTAL_ALLOC_DELTA=268436168, followed by the verifier success signal.
verification_method: verify.sh copies the mounted target repository into a writable workspace, injects the bundled Go probe under cmd/evohunt-body-probe, runs go run ./cmd/evohunt-body-probe 64, and verifies both the exact 64 MiB body/token sizes and heap-allocation thresholds that distinguish full-body buffering from a harmless parse failure.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run emitted BODY_BYTES=67108864 and TOKEN_BYTES=67108864, confirming full extraction of the oversized form-body token.
The run emitted HEAP_ALLOC_AFTER_GC=134364000, showing large retained heap while the request context stayed live.
The run emitted TOTAL_ALLOC_DELTA=268436168 and ended with [evohunt] SUCCESS: observed full-body buffering and multi-copy heap growth.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
